tours.heading: Visit Our Bees
tours.subtitle: An unforgettable experience in the heart of Lebanese nature.
tours.hero_subtitle: Suit up, meet the bees, and taste fresh honey straight from the hive.
tours.step1_desc: Make your way to our family apiary nestled in the hills of Barhalioun, Northern Lebanon.
tours.step2_desc: Get fitted with professional beekeeper gear: full suit, gloves, and veil.
tours.step3_desc: Your guide will open the hives and show you the bees at work: the queen, the workers, and the honeycomb.
tours.step4_desc: Sample multiple honey varieties straight from the hive. Nothing compares to this freshness.
tours.step5_desc: Hear stories of Lebanese beekeeping passed down through three generations.
tours.step6_desc: Leave with a jar of fresh honey, great photos, and memories to last a lifetime.
tours.faq_wear_a: Wear long pants and closed toe shoes. We provide the full beekeeper suit, gloves, and veil. Avoid dark colors and strong perfumes.
tours.faq_children_a: Yes! Children aged 6 and above are welcome. They'll be fully suited up and supervised throughout the experience.
tours.faq_rain_a: If weather conditions don't allow a safe hive visit, we'll reschedule your tour at no extra charge. We'll contact you 24 hours in advance.
tours.faq_distance_q: How far is it from Beirut?
tours.faq_distance_a: Barhalioun is approximately 100 km north of Beirut, about a 1.5 to 2 hour drive via the coastal highway.
tours.faq_buy_q: Can I buy honey on site?
tours.faq_buy_a: Absolutely! We have our full range of honey available for purchase at the apiary.
tours.faq_directions_a: We'll send you detailed directions and a map pin after you book. The apiary is easily accessible by car.
tours.faq_camera_q: Can I bring my own camera?
tours.faq_camera_a: Of course! Just make sure it's secure inside the bee suit. We also take photos for you during the tour.
tours.faq_allergy_a: If you have a known allergy to bee stings, please consult your doctor before booking. We keep an EpiPen on site for emergencies.

about.heading: Our Story
about.section1_text:
In the early 1980s, in the quiet village of Barhalioun in Northern Lebanon, our family placed its first beehives among the wild thyme and oak forests of the mountains. What began as a humble tradition passed from father to son has grown into a lifelong passion, a deep bond between our family, our land, and our bees.

Three generations later, we still tend our hives by hand, the same way our grandfather taught us. Every jar of Dany's Bees honey carries the story of our family and the terroir of our mountains.
about.section2_text:
Barhalioun sits at an altitude where the Mediterranean climate meets the mountain air, creating a unique ecosystem rich in flora: wild thyme (zaatar), oak (sindian), hawthorn, citrus orchards, and endless meadows of wildflowers. Our bees forage freely across this diverse landscape, producing honey with a complexity and depth of flavor that can only come from this land.

The Northern Lebanon highlands are one of the most biodiverse regions in the Mediterranean basin. This richness translates directly into our honey. Each variety tells the story of the flowers the bees visited, the season, and the altitude.
about.section3_text:
We believe great honey should be left alone. Our extraction process is simple and traditional: we harvest the frames by hand, spin them gently in a centrifuge, strain out the wax, and jar the honey. Nothing added, nothing removed. No heating, no ultra filtering, no blending with imported honey.

Every jar is 100% raw, unfiltered, and traceable to our own hives. You can taste the difference.
about.section4_text:
At Dany's Bees, the welfare of our bees always comes first. We never overharvest. We always leave enough honey for the colony to thrive through winter. We use no chemicals or antibiotics in our hives. Our beekeeping is sustainable, ethical, and rooted in respect for nature.

When you buy from us, you're not just getting honey. You're supporting a family tradition, a local ecosystem, and a way of life that has endured for generations.
about.pull_quote: We take care of the bees, and the bees take care of us.

trust.spoon: Free Honey Spoon with Every Jar

story_teaser.label: OUR STORY
story_teaser.heading: Three Generations of Beekeeping
story_teaser.text: In the hills of Barhalioun, Northern Lebanon, our family has been keeping bees since the 1980s. What started as a grandfather's passion has become a way of life: tending hives by hand, harvesting pure raw honey, and sharing it with families who care about what they eat.
